sectionsUnavailable: Calibrated glucose, diabetes, insulin-resistance, lab, diet-history, supplement, medication, metabolic, and ancestry-applicability models are unavailable.
Public sample PDF rows are included only as output-format evidence and are not local personal results.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: A derived MCM6/LCT rs4988235 CT call is available for lactase-persistence context. This is not a glucose prediction, diabetes diagnosis, insulin-resistance result, lab interpretation, diet recommendation, supplement recommendation, medication recommendation, or treatment claim.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: This marker can provide lactose-related context, but symptoms, ancestry, diet history, labs, medications, medical history, environment, and professional review matter for nutrition and glucose decisions.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: The fixture intentionally marks calibrated glucose, diabetes, insulin-resistance, lab, diet-history, supplement, medication, metabolic, and ancestry-applicability models unavailable.
resultRows.sourceLabel: Blood glucose, HbA1c, diabetes-risk, supplement, and BMI references
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: This local run can explain why evidence is missing, but it must not infer glucose levels, HbA1c, diabetes risk, insulin resistance, nutrient needs, food lists, diet plans, supplements, medication changes, diagnoses, or treatment from genotype alone.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: The public sample opens as a Blood Glucose Insight report and shows blood glucose report ID number 90000022222.
resultRows.description: Use this as observed report identity and public sample boundary only; it is not local blood glucose, HbA1c, diabetes, or nutrition evidence.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Extracted from page 1 of the public GenoPalate Blood Glucose Insight sample PDF; this is observed sample report identity.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: Top priorities are limiting total fat, limiting saturated fat, including complex carbohydrates, limiting added sugar, and including plenty of zinc.
resultRows.description: The summary says the sample person may respond better to a lower-fat, higher-quality carbohydrate approach, reducing added sugars, and getting enough zinc from food or supplements.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Extracted from page 2 of the public sample PDF; do not convert sample priorities into local diet or supplement advice.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: Summary targets shown: carbohydrates 50-58% of daily calories, fiber 25 g/day, added sugar under 10%, total fat 23-31%, saturated fat under 8%, protein over 25%, magnesium 310 mg/day, and zinc 9-17 mg/day.
resultRows.description: The page states recommendations are tailored to genetics, age, and sex; local output must not reuse these sample targets as personal recommendations without validated local evidence.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Extracted from page 3 of the public sample PDF; this summary contains sample output-format targets only.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: Carbohydrate recommendation: 50-58% of daily calories or 414-471 g/day; the sample links IRS1 GP000002 CC to being more likely to decrease insulin resistance on a high-carbohydrate intake.
resultRows.description: The sample includes complex carbohydrate guidance, a food-score table, and a marker table for the IRS1 gene.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Extracted from pages 4-5 of the public sample PDF; this is observed sample content, not local carbohydrate, insulin, or diet advice.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: Fiber detail page target is 14-19 g/day, while the summary page lists 25 g/day; NOTCH2 GP000007 is not detected, TCF7L2 GP000005 CT and ZBED3 GP000006 AG align with typical dietary guidelines.
resultRows.description: The sample includes snack and fiber supplement wording plus a gene table; preserve the page-level discrepancy as sample evidence rather than resolving it.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Extracted from pages 3 and 6 of the public sample PDF; do not infer local fiber needs, supplement needs, glucose response, or diet changes.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: Added sugar recommendation: less than 10% of daily calories; TCF7L2 GP000005 CT is linked in the sample to being more likely to decrease fasting glucose levels on a low-added sugar intake.
resultRows.description: The sample includes sugar-sweetened beverage swaps, common added-sugar sources, and a TCF7L2 marker row.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Extracted from page 7 of the public sample PDF; this is observed sample structure, not local fasting glucose or diet advice.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: Total fat recommendation: 23-31% of daily calories or 84-109 g/day; the visible detail page repeats IRS1 GP000002 CC rows linked to lower insulin resistance on a low-fat intake.
resultRows.description: The gene appendix additionally shows IRS1, GIPR, PPM1K, and TCF7L2 rows for total fat; the detail-page repeated IRS1 rows are preserved as sample output evidence.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Extracted from pages 8, 15, and 16 of the public sample PDF; this is observed sample content, not local fat-response, insulin, lab, or diet advice.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: Saturated fat recommendation: under 8% of daily calories or under 30 g/day; TCF7L2 GP000005 CT is linked in the sample to lower insulin resistance on a low-saturated-fat intake.
resultRows.description: The sample includes common saturated-fat sources, swap examples, and a TCF7L2 marker row.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Extracted from pages 9 and 16 of the public sample PDF; this is observed sample content, not local saturated-fat or insulin-resistance advice.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: Protein summary page target is over 25% of daily calories, while the detail page target is 23-31% or 84-109 g/day; DHCR7 GP000010 shows CC on the detail page but GG in the appendix.
resultRows.description: The sample includes protein food examples and GenoBlend text. Preserve the target and DHCR7 genotype mismatch as extraction notes.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Extracted from pages 3, 10, and 16 of the public sample PDF; do not infer local protein needs, supplement needs, cholesterol, glucose, or treatment conclusions.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: Magnesium recommendation: 310 mg/day; the detail page spells TRPM6 as TPRM6 and shows GP000060 not detected and GP000061 CC aligning with typical dietary guidelines.
resultRows.description: The appendix uses TRPM6 and describes magnesium and calcium ion-channel context; the typo is preserved as observed sample output.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Extracted from pages 11 and 16 of the public sample PDF; this is sample output structure, not local magnesium, supplement, lab, or glucose advice.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: Zinc recommendation: 9-17 mg/day; SLC30A8 GP000059 AA is linked in the sample to being more likely to decrease fasting glucose levels on a high-zinc intake.
resultRows.description: The sample includes oyster and food-score wording plus a SLC30A8 marker row.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Extracted from pages 12 and 16 of the public sample PDF; this is observed sample content, not local zinc, supplement, fasting-glucose, diet, or treatment advice.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: The sample includes grocery categories, blood glucose-friendly recipe CTA, exercise, sleep, stress, gene appendix, insulin resistance, HOMA-IR, blood glucose ranges, HbA1c ranges, and glossary sections.
resultRows.description: These sections define the output structure and educational appendix style; they do not supply local labs, diagnoses, treatment plans, or personal lifestyle prescriptions.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Extracted from pages 13-19 of the public sample PDF; keep food lists and lifestyle text separate from local recommendations.

glucoseContext: Use this validation result to verify local-agent packaging and citation behavior, not to make personal blood glucose, HbA1c, diabetes, insulin-resistance, diet, supplement, medication, or treatment decisions.
Discuss glucose labs, symptoms, medications, diet history, supplement use, and metabolic-health questions with a qualified clinician or dietitian.
Keep the GenoPalate sample nutrient targets, food lists, recipes, lifestyle text, and marker rows separate from local evidence unless an equivalent validated local model is supplied.

uncertainty: No calibrated glucose model: the fixture does not provide a validated model for blood glucose, HbA1c, diabetes risk, insulin resistance, nutrient targets, diet response, supplement needs, medication context, or ancestry applicability.
Sample report boundary: the Blood Glucose Insight sample rows are public sample output structure only; they are not local genotypes, lab values, diagnoses, or personal recommendations.

Preserved sample inconsistencies: the sample contains fiber summary versus detail targets, protein summary versus detail targets, a DHCR7 detail-versus-appendix genotype mismatch, and a TRPM6/TPRM6 spelling mismatch.
missingInputs: Validated blood glucose, HbA1c, diabetes-risk, and insulin-resistance model calibration
Fasting glucose, HbA1c, fasting insulin, HOMA-IR, glucose tolerance, random glucose, and other personal lab values
Diet history, age/sex context, ancestry applicability metadata, medications, supplement use, symptoms, medical history, and clinician or dietitian review
Validated mapping from GenoPalate internal GP marker IDs to public variant identifiers
limitations: This validation result uses derived evidence only; raw genome data is not included.

The report is educational and does not predict blood glucose, diagnose diabetes, infer insulin resistance, interpret labs, prescribe diets, recommend supplements, change medications, or provide treatment guidance.

The public GenoPalate Blood Glucose Insight sample PDF is source-bound for format parity; sample nutrient targets, food lists, recipes, lifestyle text, glucose metric ranges, and genotypes are not local results.
Internal marker IDs such as GP000002 are preserved as observed sample report identifiers and are not treated as validated rsID mappings.

Reference resources used for boundary language include MedlinePlus lactose intolerance, MedlinePlus MCM6, nutritional genomics review, GWAS Catalog, MedlinePlus DTC result limits, blood glucose and HbA1c test resources, CDC diabetes risk factors, FDA dietary supplement context, NIH ODS supplement fact sheets, CDC BMI, and the Blood Glucose Insight sample PDF.
